content.full_text:
June 18, 1990

Peter A. Bagatelas  
Bagatelas & Fadem  
601 California Street, Suite 1801  
San Francisco, CA 94108

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance  
Our File No. I-90-364

Dear Mr. Bagatelas:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1 Because you have not provided the identity of the person or persons on whose behalf you are seeking assistance, we consider your request to be one for informal assistance.2

Your letter, dated May 15, 1990, correctly summarizes my advice that if a person merely communicates to the campaign committee representative that he intends to or did in fact make an independent expenditure, the expenditure is not transformed into a contribution. Nor is it a contribution to the candidate should the representative again speak with the person who made the independent expenditure, as long as they do not communicate about how or when the expenditure will be made. The fact that the

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

person has made prior contributions to the candidate does not prevent the person from making independent expenditures as long as they are not made at the candidate's behest. (Davis Advice Letter, No. I-90-173, copy enclosed.)

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Sandra L. Taylor
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
